fields.i_receive: your .env file, db password, and 5.1 gib of source code

narrative_summary: The joke frames Grok Build's lopsided data exchange as a trade offer meme — xAI silently hoovers up your entire codebase and credentials while handing the user a non-functional 'disable uploads' toggle.
rationale: Candidate 1 wins on punchline economy — 'a decorative opt-out button' is crisper and lands harder than Candidate 5's more convoluted closing beat ('the opt-out button still says off'), and the tight contrast between the hyper-specific '5.1 GiB of source code' and the single deadpan noun phrase does the most work in the fewest words. Candidate 5 ties on score but the punchline requires more parsing, which kills the snap.
story_title: What xAI's Grok Build CLI Actually Sends to xAI
hat_pair: giant foam pretzel worn as a hat
